name: Goldman Sachs
ai_strategy_summary: Goldman Sachs treats its AI platform as the most important infrastructure investment since electronic trading. The GS AI Assistant — running on multiple approved LLMs (OpenAI GPT-4, Google Gemini, and open-source models, all hosted internally) — was deployed to all 46,500 employees in June 2025 after a 10,000-person pilot. Adoption exceeds 50% with a target of 100% by 2026. Early results show ~20% productivity lifts in key functions. The platform is designed for document drafting, code generation, client analysis, and research synthesis. CIO Marco Argenti has taken a measured approach, prioritising internal hosting and data security over speed. Goldman's broader thesis: the AI industry is transitioning from capex (building infrastructure) to tangible productivity gains — and Goldman is positioning to capture that on both the investment banking and wealth management sides.
headline_metric: 46,500 employees on GS AI platform; >50% adoption; ~20% productivity lift; 100% usage target by 2026
headline_initiative: GS AI Assistant (multi-LLM: GPT-4 + Gemini + open-source, internally hosted)

advisor_productivity.headline: GS AI Assistant deployed to 46,500 employees with ~20% productivity lift in key functions
advisor_productivity.detail: The GS AI Assistant is a centralized, secure generative AI tool deployed across Goldman's entire global workforce. It enables document drafting, research synthesis, code generation, and client query handling. Multiple LLMs (GPT-4, Gemini, open-source) are hosted internally for data security. After a 10,000-person pilot, firm-wide deployment began in June 2025. Over 50% of employees now use it daily, with 100% adoption targeted by 2026.
evidence: 46,500+ employees with access to GS AI Assistant (CNBC, Jan 2025; Fortune, June 2025)
~20% productivity lift in key functions (Nanonets analysis)
Multiple LLMs hosted internally: GPT-4, Gemini, open-source (PYMNTS)

research_content.headline: AI-powered research synthesis and document generation across investment banking and wealth management
research_content.detail: The GS AI Assistant enables research analysts and wealth advisors to synthesise reports, draft client communications, and query internal knowledge bases using natural language. The tool is positioned as a horizontal capability across all divisions rather than a wealth-management-specific product.
evidence: Research synthesis and document drafting capabilities confirmed (Fortune, CNBC)
Client query handling and knowledge retrieval (Nanonets analysis)
Cross-divisional deployment — same tool for IB, WM, and AM (Goldman Sachs insights page)

client_personalization.headline: AI-driven client insights for personalised product recommendations and financial advice
client_personalization.detail: Goldman Sachs is leveraging AI to transform customer data into actionable insights — personalised product recommendations, financial advice, and timely communications reflecting each client's needs. This is part of the broader push to modernise digital wealth platforms and scale consumer-facing offerings.
evidence: AI for personalised product recommendations and financial advice (DigitalDefynd case study)
Customer-centricity push aligned with digital wealth platform modernisation (YourStory coverage)
